A.name: Small-town / Campus Transportation Gap
A.description: Documented gap in commercial rideshare (Uber/Lyft) availability and pricing in small-town / university settings; accessibility / coverage limits; rural + campus mobility studies.

B.name: Peer-to-Peer Ridesharing & Sharing-Economy Trust
B.description: P2P ridesharing empirical studies, sharing-economy trust signals, identity verification in Airbnb/Uber-style platforms, stranger-to-stranger trust formation.

C.name: Grassroots / Informal Coordination Platforms
C.description: WeChat/WhatsApp group coordination, self-organized messaging-app communities, informal digital infrastructure, bottom-up community coordination.

D.name: International Students Digital Practices in US Universities
D.description: Chinese / international student WeChat usage, digital acculturation on American campuses, international student mobility and community practices.

E.name: Identity Verification & .edu-Scoped Platforms
E.description: Campus-scoped platform identity verification, .edu email as trust signal, closed-community identity design, institutional verification on online platforms.

F.name: Safety in Shared Mobility
F.description: Real-time location sharing, SOS / emergency button design, women-safety in rideshare, risk-mitigation patterns in shared mobility.

G.name: Gamification in Coordination / Mobility
G.description: Points-based incentives in carpooling, gamification of sustainable transport, rewards for behavioral change, point-system design for coordination.

H.name: Rating & Reputation System Design & Fairness
H.description: Rideshare rating fairness, peer reputation under algorithmic management, rating bias in platform-mediated work, peer feedback design in online platforms.

I.name: Integrated Community Platforms & Super-Apps
I.description: Super-app literature, multi-module platform studies, campus one-stop platform design, closed-community integrated services.

J.name: Algorithmic Management & Platform Labor Critique (Adversarial)
J.description: Algorithmic management of gig workers, platform labor critique, rideshare driver precarity, algorithmic opacity and worker wellbeing. Used primarily for Beat 7 adversarial scoping.

_note: 10 categories (A-J). K from the prior L_auth taxonomy has been removed. Every paper should map to one primary category plus optional secondary categories; Category J is required for Beat 7 adversarial scoping.
